steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content:
At the intersection of transversal $t$ with line $m$, angle $x°$ is located on the left side of $t$.

At the intersection of transversal $t$ with line $n$, angle $33°$ is located on the right side of $t$.

Both angles lie in the interior region between the two parallel lines.

This makes them co-interior angles (also called consecutive interior angles or same-side interior angles).

steps.title: Apply the parallel lines property
steps.content:
When a transversal intersects two parallel lines, co-interior angles are supplementary — they sum to $180°$.

$$x° + 33° = 180°$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Subtract $33°$ from both sides:

$$x = 180° - 33° = 147°$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
